title: cephaelis phyllocalymma
intro: Cephaelis phyllocalymma, also known as wild ipecac, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its roots, which contain a number of alkaloids, including emetine and cephaeline, that have been used to induce vomiting and treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cephaelis phyllocalymma include its ability to treat symptoms of malaria, amoebic dysentery, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use in treating nausea and diarrhea. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Cephaelis phyllocalymma have included the roots being dried and made into a powder or infusion, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
